person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Margaret, Countess of Buchan was the daughter of Fergus, 4th Earl of Buchan. She succeeded to the title of 5th Countess of Buchan sometime before 1211.

Margaret married William Comyn, lord of Badenoch, sometime before 1212 when he claimed the earldom of Buchan in her right. 

«b»There were at least six children from this marriage:«/b»
1.) Alexander Comyn, 6th earl of Buchan; m. Elizabeth Quincy; d. 1289

2.) Sir William Comyn

3.) Fergus Comyn

4.) Idonea Comyn; m. Gilbert de Haya

5.) Elizabeth Comyn; m. bef. 1233 William, earl of Mar; d. 1267

6.) Agnes Comyn; m. Philip Meldrum of that Ilk

Margaret, countess of Buchan, died between 8 April 1242 (when she participated in the settlement of a dispute with the Abbot of Arbroath) and 1244 (when her son Alexander succeeded to the earldom of Buchan).
_______________________________________________________________
Clan crest: Sunflower turning to the sun
Clan Buchan is a Scottish clan. The Clan Buchan are of no relation to the Clan Buchanan despite their similar name. The name Buchan is also considered a sept of the Clan Cumming (Comyn).
Origins of the Clan
The name Buchan is derived from the district of Buchan, in the north east of Aberdeenshire and Banffshire.
The ancient Mormaerdom and Earldom of Buchan came into the hands of the Clan Comyn, but they later lost it after they were defeated by Robert the Bruce.
The title of Earl of Buchan then went to Alexander Stewart, Earl of Buchan, who was also known as the Wolf of Badenoch.
The geographical name of Buchan was used by notable inhabitants of the district even though they may not have had a provable connection to the Earls of Buchan.
The historian, Black, lists one Ricardus de Buchan as a clerk to the bishopric of Aberdeen in 1207.
Before 1281 William Buchan held land in Aberdeen. 
In 1296 Sir Thomas de Boghan appears in the Ragman Rolls giving homage to Edward I of England.
Sir Thomas's lands were around Edinburgh, and his seal includes an eight rayed figure which may represent the shining sun, which also forms part of the clan chief's crest.
It is not known definitively when the Buchans gained their lands of Auchmacoy.
However, in 1446 Andrew Buchan of Achmakwy was amongst those appointed to settle the boundaries of the lands of St Peter's Hospital.
Auchmacoy is believed to have been in the possession of the family from the beginning of the 14th century.
However it was not until 1503 when Andrew Buchan, reckoned the second chief of Clan Buchan, received a charter for the lands from James IV of Scotland. Wikipedia

zh.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan是Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan的女兒，於1184年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵。她於大約1215年與William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1244年在她的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan是Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan的女儿，于1184年出生在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵。她于大约1215年与William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1244年在她的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵。

nameMeanings.name: Buchan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a district north of Aberdeen. There was also a barony of Buchquane in Strathore, Fife, a settlement called Buchan in Kirkcudbrightshire, and several other places in Scotland so named, but these are less likely to be the source of the surname.
Americanized form of Croatian and Serbian Bućan or of its Croatian variant Bučan: from a hypocoristic derivative of the personal names Budimir, Budislav, or Budivoj (see Budin ).
Americanized form of Slovenian Bučan: topographic name derived from buč ‘well in the field’, or a habitational name from the village called Buč, from the same word.

variants.name: Budin
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Budy, the name of a village in Ukraine and another in Belarus.
Slovenian and Croatian: from the personal name Budin, a short form of (Old) Slavic compound names based on the element bud ‘to inspire’ (from buditi), as for example Budislav ‘inspiring glory’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan was born in 1184, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland as the daughter of Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. She married William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan about 1215, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1244, in her hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan est née en 1184, à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, fille de Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. Elle a épousé William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan environ 1215, à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1244, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan wurde 1184 in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, als Tochter von Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan geboren. Sie hat um 1215 in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1244 im Alter von 60 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan è nata il nel 1184 nel Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland come figlia di Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. Lei sposato William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan circa 1215, nel Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1244, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchanは1184年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンでFergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchanの娘として生まれた。約1215年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンでWilliam Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1244年に故郷で60歳で死去し，スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan(은)는 1184년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1215년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1244년 그녀의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan nasceu em 1184, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland filha de Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. Ela casou-se com William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan aproximadamente 1215, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1244, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan родилась в 1184 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия. Её отца звали Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan около 1215 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1244 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Countess Margaret Buchan nació en 1184, en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland como hija de Fergus Colban Buchan 4th Earl of Buchan. Se casó con William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan aproximadamente 1215, en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1244, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.
